finding_results.notes: Draco's standalone 'Mm' (calibration-only example line) replaced with a behavioral beat: 'He nodded, already back in the text.' Preserves the muted acknowledgment and domestic ease without reproducing the example line. Harry's 'Are you sleeping?' (verbatim opening of his calibration example line) replaced with 'Getting enough sleep?' — same direct sleep-check function, different phrasing. Both replacements verified against all character example_lines in the style bible; no verbatim or near-verbatim matches remain at either position.

summary: Single finding addressed: two style-bible example lines used verbatim in the chapter (Draco's 'Mm.' and Harry's 'Are you sleeping?') replaced with fresh phrasings that perform identical dramatic work. Draco's minimal acknowledgment is now behavioral rather than vocal; Harry's sleep check uses a different formulation. All must-land beats, setups (mark_resonance_onset), state deltas, and character voice signatures preserved. No collateral changes to surrounding dialogue or chapter structure.
